red_baseline.reason: T021 isolates missing/expired operation result mapping while every recovery/no-replay path already passes.
red_baseline.summary: 4/5 pass; MCP maps missing/expired to scope_not_resolved and CLI returns no nested Agent result.

validation.summary: The inherited recovery suite passes 5/5 across real CLI/MCP response loss, correlation, missing/expired, and indeterminate branches.
26/26 focused Kernel journal, correlation, fault, policy, and CLI schema tests pass.
Syntax check passes for 96 JavaScript files and whitespace validation reports no errors.

review.evidence: operation.get/reconcile remain the only project-optional recovery routes; handlers convert journal read failures to effect-free Agent failures; CLI preserves schemaVersion 1 and nonzero exit; no handler replay, broad list, or recovery journal record was introduced.

obligation_evidence.evidence: CLI and MCP missing/expired routes now share the same Kernel operation handler and physical journal authority.
CLI failures use schemaVersion 1 failure envelopes with nested Agent results and stable nonzero exit behavior.
Recovery changes neither adapter nor managed-resource lifetime and do not create Plugin-local state.
Known/lost recovery suite passes with get/reconcile or one bounded list and never calls the original handler.
Same ID reads original evidence and digest-shaped extra authority remains input_invalid without writes.
Missing and expired IDs now return operation_record_missing_or_expired on both surfaces with effects none and no new record.
Response-loss external effect and journal counters remain exactly one after all recovery calls.
Zero/ambiguous correlation still carries no retry action and list bounds remain schema-enforced.
Insufficient indeterminate foreground evidence remains unresolved and its counter remains one through CLI/MCP reconciliation.
